# Gita — The Bhagavad Gita in English

> The complete Bhagavad Gita, all 700 verses in English. The dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna on duty, action, and the self. Free to read, share, and reuse. Public-domain translation.

Live site: https://chiragmirani.github.io/gita-quotes/
About: https://chiragmirani.github.io/gita-quotes/about.html
Machine-readable dataset: https://chiragmirani.github.io/gita-quotes/data.json

## What this is

The complete Bhagavad Gita, presented one verse at a time in canonical order, with:

- The English translation by Shri Purohit Swami (1935, public domain), with Swami Sivananda's translation as a fallback.
- The original Sanskrit in Devanagari.
- IAST transliteration.
- Sequential reading with previous and next, plus jump-to-first and jump-to-last buttons, a chapter browser for all 18 chapters, a verse-of-the-day selector, and random verse for discovery.
- Deep linking to any specific verse via ?v=BG{chapter}.{verse} (e.g. ?v=BG2.47 for the famous Karma Yoga verse).
- Installable as a standalone app on iOS and Android home screens.
- Offline support after the first visit via service worker.

## Attribution and license

Translations: Shri Purohit Swami (primary, 1935 public domain) and Swami Sivananda (fallback, widely free to quote). Source data is pulled from vedicscriptures.github.io.

Any verse or the entire dataset from this site is free for anyone to read, share, or reuse. Attribution to the translator is appreciated but not required.

## Built by

Chirag Mirani. Other public projects: MacroForecastbyCM (https://chiragmirani.github.io/macro-dashboard/), GitHub (https://github.com/ChiragMirani), Kaggle (https://www.kaggle.com/cmirani).
